title: erythroxylum mexicanum
intro: Erythroxylum mexicanum, also known as the coca plant, is a plant native to South America and has been used for centuries for its medicinal and stimulant properties. The leaves of the coca plant contain alkaloids, including cocaine and other compounds that have been used to alleviate altitude sickness, fatigue, and other conditions. Medicinally, coca plant extracts have been used to treat symptoms of altitude sickness, such as headaches and nausea, and have also been used to reduce stress and improve mental clarity. In herbal preparations, the leaves of the coca plant can be brewed into a tea, or dried and used in capsules, although the use of coca plant extracts is heavily regulated in many countries due to the presence of cocaine.
